_doc: IPEx/RASSOR reconfigurable-morphology POSTURES, data-driven for easy editing. Each posture sets the two drum-arm pitches (and the resulting chassis lift + camera vantage) so the Godot 8-camera rig and the kinematics/stability layer can render and reason about posture-conditioned observation geometry (the ARGUS subsystem capability: posture changes observation geometry/observability). The posture SET is sourced from PRD 7.4 AM-01 (TRANSIT/DIG/DUMP_Z/MEERKAT/DRUM_WALK/IRON_CROSS/SELF_RIGHT/BRAKED_HOLD) plus COBRA, and from documented RASSOR/IPEx maneuvers (NTRS 20210021455 action vocabulary; NASA RASSOR mobility demonstrations).
provenance: Posture SET: SOURCED (PRD 7.4 AM-01 + RASSOR/IPEx maneuvers). Joint ANGLES + chassis_lift: [ASSUMPTION] geometric targets for calibration, NOT measured flight values -- edit per measured IPEx kinematics. Stability gating is real (terrain_authority/stability.py SSA tip-over).

units.camera_vantage: m above the nominal TRANSIT camera height

convention: arm pitch is about the rover-local joint axis (sidecar ROVER_JOINT_AXIS = local -Z, Y-up), matching sidecar.gd ARM_FRONT_PITCH/ARM_BACK_PITCH. Larger positive = drum lifted clear of the surface; near 0.20 = drum near surface (the DIG default); negative = drum rotated DOWN below the wheels (used to plant the arms and push the chassis up, e.g. MEERKAT).

nominal.comment: the sidecar's current hardcoded stance (DIG-ish front, raised back); TRANSIT below lifts both clear.

TRANSIT.description: Driving. Both drum arms raised clear of the front-stereo FOV (mirrors sidecar _drums_up).
TRANSIT.provenance: [ASSUMPTION] matches the sidecar drums-up clear-FOV stance

DIG.description: Front drum lowered to the surface to excavate; back arm raised clear.
DIG.stability: nominal; counter-rotating drums cancel horizontal dig reaction (KSC-TOPS-7)

DUMP_Z.description: Front arm rotated up high to dump spoil over a berm (drum lifted and tipped).
DUMP_Z.stability: check fore/aft margin under the raised drum mass

MEERKAT.description: Arms rotated down under the chassis to PUSH THE BODY UP, raising the camera vantage for multi-height parallax and shadow/rock disambiguation (PRD AM-03 / SN-09).
MEERKAT.stability: speed-limited; REJECT if SSA stability margin inadequate (AM-03)
MEERKAT.provenance: [ASSUMPTION] arms-down body-lift; lift magnitude is a calibration target, not measured

DRUM_WALK.description: Arms used as legs in an alternating gait to walk over/through terrain the wheels cannot.
DRUM_WALK.stability: dynamic; alternating support polygon -- gated by stability.py per phase
DRUM_WALK.provenance: [ASSUMPTION] alternating arm-gait snapshot (one phase); full gait is a sequence

IRON_CROSS.description: Both arms extended out horizontally (cross shape) for wheel-cleaning / recovery under raised-posture safety limits (PRD AM-06).

SELF_RIGHT.description: Arms swung to flip the rover back upright after a tip-over.
SELF_RIGHT.stability: recovery maneuver; not a steady stance

BRAKED_HOLD.description: Arms planted for a stable static hold (e.g., during a long observation dwell or charge).
BRAKED_HOLD.stability: maximally stable static stance; widest support polygon

COBRA.description: One arm raised high (reach / close inspection of a feature ahead), the other lowered for support.
COBRA.stability: check fore/aft margin under the high reaching arm
